key_additions: get_scan_timestamp() call per symbol before review
Human-readable timestamp formatting with minutes-ago calculation
Last scan info injected into print output

patterns_discovered.description: StateManager used to persist and retrieve scan timestamps across execution cycles without modifying core review logic
Timestamps converted to 'X minutes ago' format with fallback to 'first scan' for initial state
New feature added with only 2-line change to print statement, keeping existing control flow intact

gotchas_discovered.description: Integer division for minutes conversion (/ 60 with int()) truncates seconds, may show '0m ago' for recent scans
Code assumes get_scan_timestamp() returns falsy value (None/0) for first scan; behavior undefined if StateManager raises exception
Each get_scan_timestamp() + calculation uses current dt.datetime.now(), creating micro-timing differences within same loop iteration

discoveries.approach_outcome: SUCCESS - Scan timestamp reporting integrated cleanly into orchestrator workflow with minimal code changes and no apparent breaking changes to existing functionality

recommendations.suggestion: Add error handling around StateManager calls to gracefully handle missing/corrupted state
recommendations.rationale: Production resilience - prevents orchestrator crash if state store unavailable

recommendations.suggestion: Cache current timestamp once at main() entry to avoid microsecond drift in reporting
recommendations.rationale: Consistency - all timestamps in single run would reflect same moment

recommendations.suggestion: Consider threshold for 'first scan' display (e.g., if last_scan < 1 minute old, show exact seconds)
recommendations.rationale: UX clarity - helps distinguish between truly new symbols and very recent scans
